fix: resolve SSR hydration ordinal drift from structural blocks (#216) * fix: resolve SSR hydration ordinal drift from structural blocks and count element/text ordinals to map compiled template paths to SSR DOM nodes. When conditional () or repeat () blocks render content into the SSR DOM, the extra nodes shift ordinals so later siblings resolve to the wrong element. Root cause: the compiled template static HTML (meta.h) strips structural blocks, but the SSR DOM contains them inline between marker pairs (... and ...). The ordinal counting did not account for these extra ranges. This manifested in the commerce app when navigating from a zero-result search page (/search?q=test) to a category: the loop's parent container resolved to the

no-results message instead of

, leaving the product grid empty after SPA navigation. Framework changes (webui-framework): - Extract findByOrdinal() into markers.ts — walks parent children counting only nodes outside structural block marker pairs, with depth tracking for nested blocks of the same type. Both (element ordinals) and (text ordinals) now delegate to this single function. - Defer SSR marker removal — closing markers (, ) and item markers () are collected during hydration but only removed after all path-based resolution is complete (after for events/refs). This ensures marker pairs are intact whenever findByOrdinal needs to skip structural blocks, regardless of hydration phase ordering. - Add 10 unit tests for findByOrdinal covering: conditional skipping, repeat skipping, nested conditionals, multiple sequential blocks, empty blocks, text ordinal drift, interleaved block types, and edge cases (empty parent, out-of-range ordinal). Commerce app changes: - Fix mp-category-nav @attr mode — change all-active from string mode (default '') to boolean mode (default false). String mode produced '' (falsy) from the boolean attribute, causing a hydration mismatch where the server-rendered block was not tracked by the client, leaving orphaned 'All' DOM nodes visible after SPA navigation. - Fix Related Products scrollbar visibility — the scrollbar thumb used --webui-border (#262626), the same color as the track (--webui-surface #262626), making it invisible. Changed to --webui-text-muted and added scrollbar-width/scrollbar-color for Firefox cross-browser support. - Add regression test for search→category SPA navigation verifying category nav state and product grid population. - Update visual regression snapshots. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * Revert --------- Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ui): use SizeChanged one-shot handler to set SplitterDistance

OnShown fires before WM_SIZE delivers a real pixel width to the
Fill-docked SplitContainer, so _split.Width == 0 there too.

Replace with a SizeChanged lambda that:
- waits until Width > Panel1MinSize + Panel2MinSize + SplitterWidth
- sets SplitterDistance to Width / 2 (50/50 split)
- immediately unsubscribes itself so it only fires once

Also remove the unreliable OnShown assignment.

commit.message:
feat(benchmarks): add conv_cifar_v2 suite for phase-2 GPU sweep

New suite with 24 configurations organized in 9 groups targeting 75%+
CIFAR-10 accuracy:

1. Multi-scale without GCN (+std, +std+hflip) — test if std alone
   beats gcn+std for multi-scale, which was the case for single-stage.
2. Alpha sweep on best single config (5e-3, 2e-3, 1e-3, 5e-4) —
   current default 1e-2 may not be optimal.
3. More neurons (15k, 20k, 30k) — memory-feasible with accumulator
   cleanup; 20k needs ~1.9 GB, 30k needs ~4.0 GB for head layer.
4. Richer SPP [1,2,4,8] — 5440-dim features vs 1344 with [1,2,4].
5. Multi-scale with 20k neurons — combine two top improvements.
6. Diverse ensemble + hflip (±gcn, ×6 and ×9) — missing combo from
   v1 that combines the best ensemble type with augmentation.
7. Multi-scale ensemble + hflip (32×3 and 64×3) — ensemble of the
   most efficient single-model architecture.
8. Multi-scale diverse ensemble — members with different multi-scale
   configs (2-scale and 3-scale mixes).
9. Best single/multi-scale + 20k + hflip — push the upper bound.

Also updated colab_launcher.ipynb with the new suite.

Fix Docker scan hang: bound container removal, surface timeout telemetry (#1778)

* Fix Docker scan hang: bound container removal, surface timeout telemetry

Fix 1: Add 30s timeout to RemoveContainerAsync in the finally block of
CreateAndRunContainerAsync. Previously used CancellationToken.None which
could hang indefinitely if the Docker daemon is slow removing a stuck
container, blocking the entire detector and pipeline.

Fix 2: Re-throw OperationCanceledException (when the token is cancelled)
before the generic catch(Exception) in ResolveImageAsync,
ScanDockerImageAsync, and ScanLocalImageAsync. This lets the OCE
propagate to ExecuteDetectorAsync where LinuxContainerDetectorTimeout
telemetry is emitted, enabling root-cause analysis of stuck scans.

Fix 3: Replace JsonSerializer.Serialize(e) with e.ToString() in
LinuxScanner.RunSyftAsync. Serializing exceptions with System.Text.Json
throws NotSupportedException on MethodBase properties.

Fix 4: Add safety-net catch(Exception) in ExecuteDetectorAsync after the
OCE catch. Ensures unexpected errors never escape the detector and crash
the harness or lose other detectors' results.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: fix test token cancellation and assertions

- Use pre-cancelled CancellationToken in OCE tests so the
  'when (cancellationToken.IsCancellationRequested)' guard is exercised
- Add Verify/assertion in multi-image test to confirm first image was scanned
- Use CancelAsync() instead of Cancel() per analyzer rules

Pass the caught exception to LogWarning so Docker daemon

commit.message:
perf(conv): free accumulators after solve to reduce GPU memory

After solve_accumulated() completes in ConvNEFPipeline.fit(), the _ata
(n_neurons × n_neurons float32) and _aty matrices are no longer needed.
Calling reset_accumulators() immediately frees this memory.

For 10k neurons this saves 400 MB per ensemble member.  For larger
neuron counts (20k+) the savings are critical — 1.6 GB+ per member.
Also added the same cleanup in the flat pixel benchmark path.

commit.message:
docs: add arcjet.guard documentation to README (#107)

Documents the `arcjet.guard` package API in the README, covering the
lower-level guard interface designed for AI agent tool calls and
background tasks where no HTTP request object is available.

- **Features list**: Added Guard entry and "AI agent tool calls" row to
the recommendation table
- **Comparison table**: How `arcjet.guard` differs from
`arcjet`/`arcjet_sync` (request object, rule binding, rate limit keys,
custom rules)
- **Per-feature sections**: Rate limiting (token bucket, fixed window,
sliding window), prompt injection, sensitive info detection, custom
rules with typed config/input/data
- **Result access patterns**: `rule.result(decision)`,
`inp.denied_result(decision)`, decision layers
- **Parameter reference**: `guard()` method signature table

Quick start example:

```py
from arcjet.guard import launch_arcjet, TokenBucket, DetectPromptInjection

aj = launch_arcjet(key=arcjet_key)
user_limit = TokenBucket(refill_rate=100, interval_seconds=60, max_tokens=1000)
prompt_scan = DetectPromptInjection()

async def handle_tool_call(user_id: str, message: str):
    decision = await aj.guard(
        label="tools.weather",
        rules=[user_limit(key=user_id, requested=5), prompt_scan(message)],
    )
    if decision.conclusion == "DENY":
        raise RuntimeError(f"Blocked: {decision.reason}")
```

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: qw-in <19194187+qw-in@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: add tarpaulin coverage job (#23)

* feat: implement Phase 3 e-commerce heuristics and related product pruning

* docs: modernize OUTPUT_CONTRACT and EXTRACTION wiki references

* feat(core): modernize extraction engine, eradicate panics, and add cloud jina opt-in

This commit resolves major architectural goals by hardening execution stability,
improving text-extraction heuristics, and enforcing privacy-by-default routing:

1. Panic Eradication & Error Handling
   - System-wide removal of `unwrap()` and `expect()`, replacing them with safe
     `Result` propagation.
   - Refactored `RipwebError` and domain errors (`DdgError`, `GithubError`, etc.)
     to leverage the `thiserror` crate.
   - Updated `main()` to return `anyhow::Result<()>`, migrating `std::process::exit()`
     calls to graceful `anyhow::bail!` error bubbling.

2. AST-Based Semantic Pruning
   - Replaced fragile character-counting link saturation heuristics with mathematical
     AST calculations via the `tl` crate (`evaluate_block_saturation`).
   - Accurately computes link density by evaluating structural DOM nodes,
     significantly improving the accuracy of sidebar and navigation pruning.

3. Privacy by Default
   - Added an explicit `--allow-cloud` CLI flag to gate third-party
     extraction proxies (Jina.ai).
   - Forces `ripweb` to strictly adhere to localized parsing and heuristic fallbacks
     unless explicitly given permission to use offsite JS rendering engines.

Also updates `CURRENT_PRIORITIES.md` to mark these milestones as completed.

commit.message:
v1.21.16: reject untrusted domains in SearXNG Strategy 2 EAN lookup

EAN numbers can appear in trademark, patent, and legal database pages.
SearXNG's Strategy 2 was blindly using those pages' titles as product names,
producing results like 'GODLY GOAL-GETTER Trademark Application' while the
Kesko CDN correctly returned the real product image.

Fix: Strategy 2 now only uses titles from a trusted-domain whitelist
(k-ruoka.fi, s-kaupat.fi, foodie.fi, barcodelookup.com, openfoodfacts.org,
etc.). Untrusted domains fall through to Strategy 3 which returns
'Unknown product (EAN)' with the correct image.

Also synced grocy_scraper_addon copy of searxng_client.py.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
artifacts: preserve best checkpoint and held-out test eval

Preserve the actual best validation checkpoint copied from Aorus:
- artifacts/models/best_model_baseline_s789.pt
- SHA256: 76907b9ea0170636dcb09a0645518ca26c77e8640f64ec2092a03d6babf110bc

Also save supporting result artifacts from Aorus:
- retrain_v4.json
- ensemble_v4.json
- error_analysis.json
- adaptive_nms.json
- push_v5.json
- p64_result.json

Add held-out test evaluation for the preserved checkpoint:
- val F1 = 0.9792, rate MAE = 0.198 bpm
- val_adv F1 = 0.9793, rate MAE = 0.183 bpm
- test F1 = 0.9110, rate MAE = 0.515 bpm

This confirms the checkpoint reproduces the strong validation score, but the
held-out patient test split is materially lower, so 0.9792 should not be used
as the generalization estimate.
